text.title: Async Error Handling and Antipatterns
text.summary: Error handling in async/await code and concrete antipatterns LLMs introduce.

antipattern.description: unwrap() or expect() in production async fn "to keep it short".
antipattern.reason: Any None or Err panics the entire Tokio task, crashing all concurrent requests sharing that thread. Use ? and propagate instead.
The save fails invisibly. At minimum, log the error; for data integrity paths, propagate with ?.

antipattern.description: Adding #[from] anyhow::Error to a thiserror enum variant in a library.
antipattern.reason: Collapses all downstream errors into one opaque variant. Callers lose the ability to match on specific failure types.

antipattern.description: From<&str> or From<String> impls on error enums.
antipattern.reason: These silently swallow context. Use #[from] on real error types only, not on string messages.

antipattern.description: IntoResponse returning 500 for every AppError variant.
antipattern.reason: Hides NotFound (should be 404), Unauthorized (401), etc. Clients cannot act on the response. Require per-variant status mapping.

rule.statement: Use panic!/expect only for programming errors (invariant violations). Use Result for all expected failure paths. Use unwrap only in tests or when the value is logically guaranteed to be Ok/Some.
rule.rationale: Panics propagate out of tokio::spawn as JoinError, not as AppError. Unguarded panics in handlers can crash the runtime depending on configuration.

text.reference: CI script banning unwrap_used, expect_used, Box<dyn Error> in public APIs
